source.journal_name: Romanian Journal of Information Science and Technology

paper.title: 112 Emergency Video Call Response Pipeline for Car Crashes Using Computer Vision and Natural Language Processing
paper.abstract: 112 emergency response pipeline integrating ASR, object detection (YOLOv8), NLP, and blockchain. Video analysis for car accidents. Speech-to-text with Whisper. Severity classification via rule-based NLP. Blockchain for vehicle owner verification.

verdict.summary: Applied ML system integrating multiple technologies for emergency response. Standard ML methodology (YOLOv8, Whisper, NLP). Novel dataset CarEM-Vid created. Formalism is standard for applied CV/NLP.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Emergency response system with CV/NLP
**Level:** 2-3 (Applied ML system integration)
**Rationale:** Integrates existing ML components (YOLOv8, Whisper, blockchain) into novel application.

### Necessity Test
- ML accuracy metrics: Standard, necessary
- NLP severity rules: Appropriate for methodology
- No set theory for list operations

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for applied ML paper.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning and Metaheuristic Approaches to Maximize Downlink Sum-Rate for Internet of Things Systems in Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access-based Space-Air-Ground Integrated Networks
paper.abstract: DRL for SAGIN-IoT optimization. Joint optimization of HAP/UAV positions, transmit powers, bandwidth allocation, and link selection. Deep Q-learning vs Alternative Optimization with Differential Annealing. LEO satellite, HAP, and UAV integration.

verdict.summary: Wireless optimization using DRL. Channel models (Eq. 1-3), SINR formulation, optimization objectives are standard and necessary for SAGIN system design. Heavy formalism appropriate for Level 3-4 optimization.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** DRL optimization for SAGIN-IoT
**Level:** 3-4 (Optimization with theoretical formulation)
**Rationale:** Novel optimization approach for complex wireless system.

### Necessity Test
- Path loss equations (Eq. 1): Necessary for wireless modeling
- Rician fading (Eq. 2): Standard channel model
- SINR formulation (Eq. 3): Necessary for optimization

**Verdict:** Formalism necessary for wireless communications optimization.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Design and Simulation of Integrated Radio Frequency Transmitter for Wireless Sensor Network Applications
paper.abstract: Low-power RF transmitter using 0.18um CMOS for 2.4GHz WSN. LC VCO with stacked NMOS/PMOS. Sub-harmonic mixer. 1V supply, 2.55mW power, IIP3 3.19dBm, 12.5dB conversion gain.

verdict.summary: CMOS RF circuit design with simulation. Transconductance equations (Eq. 1-5) and AC analysis are standard and necessary for analog IC design. Domain-appropriate formalism.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** RF transmitter IC design
**Level:** 3 (Circuit design)
**Rationale:** Novel VCO + mixer integration for WSN.

### Necessity Test
- Transconductance equations: Necessary for circuit analysis
- Input resistance derivation: Necessary for impedance matching
- Phase noise analysis: Standard for VCO design

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for analog IC design.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Miniaturized High Frequency Ratio Quadrature Coupler for Dual Band Wireless Systems
paper.abstract: Dual-band branch-line coupler with 8.28 frequency ratio. 0.7 GHz and 5.6 GHz operation. Miniaturized impedance inverter TLs. Return loss >30dB, insertion/coupling ~3.5dB. Size 0.45λg×0.23λg.

verdict.summary: RF coupler design with fabrication and measurement. Impedance transformation equations (Eq. 1) are necessary for dual-band TL design. Fabricated and measured prototype validates theory.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Dual-band microwave coupler
**Level:** 3 (RF/microwave engineering)
**Rationale:** Novel design achieving highest frequency ratio reported.

### Necessity Test
- Impedance inverter equations (Eq. 1): Necessary for dual-band design
- S-parameter measurements: Standard validation

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for RF engineering.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: AI Texts under the Etymologic Lens
paper.abstract: Etymological analysis of AI-generated texts vs human literary texts. ChatGPT tested on Romanian novel La Medeleni. AI fails to replicate etymological distribution of human author. Tool for distinguishing AI from human texts.

verdict.summary: Novel approach to AI text detection via etymology. Chi-square tests and Zipf law analysis are standard statistical methods, necessary for comparative analysis. No decorative formalism.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** AI text detection via etymology
**Level:** 2 (Computational linguistics)
**Rationale:** Novel application of etymological analysis.

### Necessity Test
- Chi-square test: Necessary for distribution comparison
- Zipf law analysis: Standard for linguistics
- No set theory for simple operations

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for statistical linguistics.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Personalized Movement Algorithms for Neural Forearm Prostheses Using Convolutional Neural Networks
paper.abstract: CNN for neural prosthesis control. Plug electrodes in median/ulnar nerves. 5DT Data Glove for training. FPGA implementation (Basys3). 31 distinct movements from 5 electrode needles. 20ms inference time.

verdict.summary: Applied ML for prosthetics. Minimal formalism - one combinatorics equation (Eq. 1) for movement count. Hardware implementation with FPGA. Appropriate for engineering application.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** CNN-based neural prosthesis
**Level:** 2-3 (Applied ML for prosthetics)
**Rationale:** System integration with hardware implementation.

### Necessity Test
- Combinatorics (Eq. 1): Simple, appropriate
- CNN architecture description: Standard

**Verdict:** Minimal formalism, appropriate for engineering.

### Issues
None identified.

batch_notes: Volume 28 Issue 4 (2025) analyzed with RUIN Framework v2.0 and Necessity Test. **No formalism theater detected.** All 6 papers have domain-appropriate formalism: emergency CV/NLP system, wireless optimization, RF circuit design, microwave coupler, computational linguistics, neural prosthetics. Final: 0 CRITICAL, 0 LIMITED, 4 ADEQUATE, 2 STRONG.
